title: POST create without Idempotency-Key — network retry creates duplicate
expected_feedback: Standard: POST /orders (create) has no idempotency mechanism. Mobile clients frequently retry requests (network timeout, app backgrounded, user double-tap). Without Idempotency-Key support, each retry creates a new order — leading to duplicate charges. Fix: support Idempotency-Key header. On first request: process normally, store response keyed by idempotency key. On retry (same key, same body): return cached response. On conflict (same key, different body): return 409.
violated_rule: Idempotency strategy exists for create/action mutations
